text: Amazon Sub is obligated to purchase on March 31, 2026, subject to certain closing conditions. The investment provided for under the Letter Agreement is separate from and in addition to Amazon Sub's agreement to invest $15.0 billion in OpenAI's Series C Preferred Stock.
NVIDIA agreed to purchase 214,776,632 shares of the Company's common stock at $23.28 per share, representing an aggregate purchase price in cash of $5.0 billion.

Intel Corporation completed the issuance and sale of 86,956,522 shares of the Company's common stock to SoftBank Group Corp. at a price per share of $23.00, for an aggregate purchase price in cash of $2.0 billion.

Marvell Technology, Inc. completed the issuance and sale of 2,000,000 shares of the Company's Series A Convertible Preferred Stock to NVIDIA Corporation for an aggregate purchase price of $2,000,000,000.00 in cash.
NVIDIA purchased 4,821,717 shares of Synopsys' common stock for an aggregate purchase price of $2 billion in cash.
On January 23, 2026, CoreWeave, Inc. entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement with NVIDIA Corporation at a price of $87.20 per share for an aggregate purchase price of $2 billion in cash.

AT&T Mobility II LLC, a subsidiary of AT&T Inc., entered into a License Purchase Agreement. Pursuant to the terms and subject to the conditions set forth in the License Purchase Agreement, the Seller Parties have agreed to sell all 3.45 GHz and 600 MHz spectrum licenses that are either licensed to or pending assignment to the Seller Parties, together with a 99-year extension of existing leases for Buyer's exclusive use of certain wireless spectrum licenses in Hawaii, for an aggregate purchase price of $22,650,000,000.00 in cash, subject to certain potential adjustments.
note: full real SEC 8-K text (not just the short excerpt production stored), https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1415404/000141540425000035/tmb-20250825x8k.htm -- this is a spectrum-license (asset) purchase, not an equity investment; production's own DB currently records it as an ownership edge, which this eval flags as a likely misclassification worth a backlog entry rather than ground truth to imitate

text: Pursuant to the terms of the Transactions, the consideration for the Transactions payable by SpaceX at the Spectrum Acquisition Closing is $17 billion.

The Company filed a prospectus supplement relating to the closing of the first tranche under the amended and restated stock purchase agreement, which provides for the issuance and sale by the Company to Toyota Motor Corporation of shares of common stock at a purchase price of $5.03 per share, for an aggregate purchase price of $250,000,000.
The Purchaser, an affiliate of Silver Lake, acquired 51% of the equity interests of Altera Corporation for an equity value of approximately $3.3 billion.

On August 22, 2025, Intel Corporation (the "Company") entered into a Warrant and Common Stock Agreement (the "Purchase Agreement") with the United States Department of Commerce (the "DOC"). Pursuant to the terms of the Purchase Agreement, the Federal Government of the United States of America (the "US Government") agreed to make a total of $8,869,800,000 of disbursements to the Company, consisting of (1) the acceleration of certain disbursements under the Company's Direct Funding Agreement with the DOC in the amount of $5,695,000,000, and (2) $3,174,800,000 of disbursements (the "Secure Enclave Disbursements") in respect of the Company's agreement with the US Government.
note: full real SEC 8-K text (fuller context than production's stored raw_excerpt), https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/50863/000005086325000129/intc-20250822.htm

text: Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25 billion per month for compute capacity under a multi-year agreement, giving Anthropic dedicated access to xAI's data center infrastructure.
note: a compute/service contract, not an investment — the kind of case the extraction prompt is meant to reject

text: Nebius has agreed to sell more than $1 billion of AI computing capacity to Reflection AI. The contract runs through 2029 and gives Reflection access to Nvidia GB300 chips.
note: real deal added manually to the graph 2026-07-14 (supplier_customer edge_type is schema-supported but extraction for it isn't wired up yet, a known v2 gap) — correct current-system behaviour is to extract zero events, since this is a compute/supply contract

text: Reflection AI will pay SpaceX $150 million per month starting July 1, 2026, for compute capacity at SpaceX's Colossus 2 data center. The deal could total up to $6.3 billion if it runs through 2029, though either party may cancel with 90 days' notice after the first three months.
note: real deal added manually to the graph 2026-07-14, same v2 scope gap as neg-02 — correct current-system behaviour is zero events

text: This prospectus relates to the initial public offering of 18,000,000 shares of our common stock. We are offering all of the shares of common stock offered by this prospectus. Prior to this offering, there has been no public market for our common stock.
note: constructed test case (not a verbatim filing) modeled on standard S-1/424B IPO-prospectus boilerplate — tests the IPO gate: a company selling shares to the public has no specific investor

text: The offering is being made through a syndicate of underwriters led by Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, as representatives of the several underwriters. Various Institutional Investors are expected to purchase the majority of the shares offered.
note: constructed test case — tests both the underwriter gate and the collective-noun gate (is_generic_entity)
